Title: Why Your Business Website Is Losing You Customers | Fix 2026
Description: Save this for your business website and digital presence board. Most small business websites lose customers within the first 8 seconds. Not because the business is bad — but because the website fails one or more of these 5 tests that every customer unconsciously runs before deciding to trust a business they found online. If you don't know which of these 5 your website fails, assume it fails at least 3. Save this and check your own website right now. #BusinessWebsite #WebDesign #SmallBusiness #DigitalPresence #WebDevelopment #CustomerExperience #BusinessTech
Alt Text: Dark editorial infographic showing 5 unconscious customer trust tests for business websites, each test with a red failure indicator and specific description, solution panel in teal, deep charcoal background — business language accessible to non-technical business owners
AI Prompt: Clean editorial infographic, 9:16 vertical, Style B Dynamic Viral. Background: deep charcoal (#0d0d1a). Title bold white: 5 TESTS YOUR WEBSITE FAILS IN THE FIRST 8 SECONDS. Subtitle amber: Every customer runs these. Most websites fail 3 or more. Below, 5 tests each with a red indicator: TEST 1 (RED) — THE PHONE TEST: Does your website work perfectly on a mobile phone? 62% of web traffic is mobile. If buttons are hard to tap, text is small, or pages load slowly — the customer is gone. TEST 2 (RED) — THE 3-SECOND CLARITY TEST: In 3 seconds, can a new visitor understand what your business does, who it's for, and how to contact you? If they have to read to find out, they won't. TEST 3 (RED) — THE TRUST TEST: Does your website show proof? (Reviews, photos of real work, real client names.) Customers trust businesses that show evidence. TEST 4 (RED) — THE SPEED TEST: Does your website load in under 3 seconds? For every 1 extra second of load time, 7% of visitors leave. A slow website is a closed door. TEST 5 (RED) — THE NEXT STEP TEST: Is there one clear action on every page? Call now, Book here, Get a quote. If there is no clear next step, customers don't take one. Bottom panel (TEAL): A professionally built website fixes all 5 — and it pays for itself in the first month of new client enquiries. Footer amber: Your website is your 24/7 salesperson. Make sure it's actually selling. Colour: charcoal, red, teal, amber. No photography.

Title: When to Build Custom vs Buy Off-the-Shelf | Business Tech Decision 2026
Description: Save this for your business technology decisions and software strategy board. Most business owners face this decision at some point: buy an existing software subscription, or invest in custom software built for your specific business. The wrong choice costs you either a year of workarounds with software that almost fits, or money spent on custom development you didn't actually need. This framework makes the decision clear. Save this before your next software purchase. #BusinessSoftware #CustomSoftware #SoftwareDecision #BusinessTech #SME #DigitalTransformation #SoftwareStrategy
Alt Text: Dark editorial infographic showing clear decision framework for when to build custom software vs buy existing off-the-shelf, using 5 criteria questions with build vs buy recommendation for each, deep charcoal background in amber-teal-red — business language for non-technical business owners
AI Prompt: Clean editorial infographic, 9:16 vertical, Style B Dynamic Viral. Background: deep charcoal (#0d0d1a). Title bold white: BUILD CUSTOM OR BUY OFF-THE-SHELF? A Simple Business Decision Framework. Subtitle amber: The right answer saves you a year of frustration. Below, 5 decision criteria each with a question and two outcomes: QUESTION 1: IS YOUR BUSINESS PROCESS STANDARD? YES → BUY (teal): Off-the-shelf works. Xero for accounting, Shopify for e-commerce, Calendly for booking. NO → BUILD (amber): Your process is your competitive advantage. Standard software will limit you. QUESTION 2: DO YOU NEED INTEGRATION WITH YOUR OTHER SYSTEMS? SIMPLE INTEGRATION → BUY (teal): Most off-the-shelf tools connect via API. COMPLEX OR PROPRIETARY → BUILD (amber): If you need deep custom integration, off-the-shelf creates more problems than it solves. QUESTION 3: WHAT IS THE COST OVER 5 YEARS? RUN THE MATH: Off-the-shelf: monthly fee × 60 months + workaround time cost. Custom: one-time build + maintenance. For many businesses, custom is cheaper after year 2. QUESTION 4: HOW IMPORTANT IS THIS SOFTWARE TO YOUR CORE BUSINESS? SUPPORTING FUNCTION → BUY (teal). CORE OPERATION → BUILD (amber): If the software is central to how you serve clients, generic tools create ceilings. QUESTION 5: DO YOU NEED FEATURES NO EXISTING TOOL HAS? NO → BUY (teal). YES → BUILD (amber): Custom is the only option. Footer amber: The worst outcome: off-the-shelf software that's 80% right, and a business workflow built around the 20% it can't do. Colour: charcoal, amber, teal. No photography.
